revision.text: #redirect[[en:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Augenbraue des Schafs'' }} '''Semed-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der nur einen [[Baktiu|Dekan-Stern]] beinhaltete und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Himmelskörper]] ist hierbei das [[Doppelstern]]system [[Algiedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] erschien Semed-seret in der Sethos-Schrift an 19. Stelle; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] wird Semed-seret als 18. Dekan geführt, dessen [[heliakisch]]er Aufgang für den [[Achet#Datierungen|26. Achet II]] angesetzt war. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Semed-seret

Der auffälligste [[Himmelskörper]] ist hierbei der optische [[Doppelstern]] [[Algiedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] erschien Semed-seret in der Sethos-Schrift an 19. Stelle; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] wird Semed-seret als 18. Dekan geführt, dessen [[heliakisch]]er Aufgang für den [[Achet#Datierungen|26. Achet II]] angesetzt war. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Augenbraue des Schafs'' }} '''Semed-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der nur einen [[Baktiu|Dekan-Stern]] beinhaltete und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Himmelskörper]] ist hierbei der optische [[Doppelstern]] [[Algiedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repäsentierte Kenemu am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 18. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|26. Achet II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Augenbraue des Schafs'' }} '''Semed-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der nur einen [[Baktiu|Dekan-Stern]] beinhaltete und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Himmelskörper]] ist hierbei der optische [[Doppelstern]] [[Algiedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Semed-seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 18. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|26. Achet II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. Aufgrund der [[Extinktion (Astronomie)|Extinktion]] ist Semed-seret bis zu einer Horizonthöhe von 3,5° sichtbar. Der scheinbare Auf- oder Untergang erfolgt gegenüber dem tatsächlichen Auf- oder Untergang etwa 21 bis 24 Minuten später beziehungsweise früher. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Augenbraue des Schafs'' }} '''Semed-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der nur einen [[Baktiu|Dekan-Stern]] beinhaltete. == Altägyptische Astronomie == Semed-sered gehörte zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]], welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Himmelskörper]] ist hierbei der optische [[Doppelstern]] [[Algiedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Semed-seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 18. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|26. Achet II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. Aufgrund der [[Extinktion (Astronomie)|Extinktion]] ist Semed-seret bis zu einer Horizonthöhe von 3,5° sichtbar. Der scheinbare Auf- oder Untergang erfolgt gegenüber dem tatsächlichen Auf- oder Untergang etwa 21 bis 24 Minuten später beziehungsweise früher. == Koordinaten von Semed-sered (Äquinoktium 1850 v. Chr.) == * [[Rektaszension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Deklination (Astronomie)|Deklination]]: -15°05'46" == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Augenbraue des Schafs'' }} '''Semed-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der nur einen [[Baktiu|Dekan-Stern]] beinhaltete. == Altägyptische Astronomie == Semed-seret gehörte zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]], welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Himmelskörper]] ist hierbei der optische [[Doppelstern]] [[Algiedi]] im Sternbild [[Steinbock (Sternbild)|Steinbock]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Semed-seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 18. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|26. Achet II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. Aufgrund der [[Extinktion (Astronomie)|Extinktion]] ist Semed-seret bis zu einer Horizonthöhe von 3,5° sichtbar. Der scheinbare Auf- oder Untergang erfolgt gegenüber dem tatsächlichen Auf- oder Untergang etwa 21 bis 24 Minuten später beziehungsweise früher. == Koordinaten von Semed-seret (Äquinoktium 1850 v. Chr.) == * [[Rektaszension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Deklination (Astronomie)|Deklination]]: −15°05'46" == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Semed-seret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{illChristian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{illChristian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. ''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[en:draft:Semed-seret]] [[de:Semed-seret]] [[sn:Semed-seret]] [[sn:emed-seret]]

Achet II|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|12=draft|12=draft}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[en:draft:Semed-seret]] [[de:Semed-seret]] [[sn:Semed-seret]] [[sn:emed-seret]]

Achet II|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|qq=draft}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|qq=draft}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|qq=draft}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|qq=draft}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern|qq=draft}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi|qq=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|qq=draft}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Semed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Semed-seret]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Semed-seret]] [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[en:draft:Semed-seret]] [[de:Semed-seret]] [[sn:emed-seret]] [[sn: failed]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|qq=draft}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|qq=draft}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|de|Doppelstern|qq=draft}} {{ill|Algedi|de|Algiedi|qq=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus (constellation)|de|Steinbock (Sternbild)|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|qq=draft}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Semed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Semed-seret]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Semed-seret]] [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[en:draft:Semed-seret]] [[de:Semed-seret]] [[sn:emed-seret]] [[sn: failed]]

The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the optical [[:en:Binary star|Binary star]] [[:en:Alpha Capricorni|Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the optical [[:en:Binary star|Binary star]] [[:en:Alpha Capricorni|Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Semed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Semed-seret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Semed-seret]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Semed-seret]] [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[de:Semed-seret]] [[sn:emed-seret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]]

Achet II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[:en:Right ascension|Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the optical [[:en:Binary star|Binary star]] [[:en:Alpha Capricorni|Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[:en:Right ascension|Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the optical [[:en:Binary star|Binary star]] [[:en:Alpha Capricorni|Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[:en:Right ascension|Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Semed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Semed-seret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Semed-seret]] }} [[sn:Semed-seret]] [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[de:Semed-seret]] [[sn:emed-seret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Semed-seret]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q1621224]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=}} is the optical [[:en:Binary star|Binary star]] [[:en:Alpha Capricorni|Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[:en:Right ascension|Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Semed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[de:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q1621224}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[de:Semed-seret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q1621224]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:10, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = S29-m-D46 D13 E11 N14 N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Semed-seret
''Smd-sr.t''
''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}} is the optical [[:en:Binary star|Binary star]] [[:en:Alpha Capricorni|Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[:en:Right ascension|Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. ''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}} is the optical [[:en:Binary star|Binary star]] [[:en:Alpha Capricorni|Algedi]] in the constellation [[:en:Capricornus|Capricornus (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[:en:Right ascension|Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Semed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[de:Semed-seret]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] {{wikidata link|Q1621224}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''.
