Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * [[:en:Right ascension|Right ascension]]: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. ''Eyebrow of the Sheep'' }} '''Semed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan star). == Ancient Egyptian Astronomy == Semed-seret belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=Constellation}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Celestial object|de|Himmelskörper|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Celestial object|qq=|WD=Q6999}} is the optical {{ill|Binary star|en|Binary star|WD=Q50053|lt=Binary star}} {{ill|Alpha Capricorni|en|Alpha Capricorni|WD=Q2646579|lt=Algedi}} in the constellation {{ill|Capricornus|en|Capricornus|WD=Q10535|lt=Capricornus (constellation)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|en|Extinction (astronomy)|WD=Q943247|lt=Extinction (astronomy)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * {{ill|Right ascension|en|Right ascension|WD=Q13442|lt=Right ascension}}: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Semed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[de:Semed-seret]] {{wikidata link|Q1621224}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, Semed-seret represented the 18th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|en|Extinction (astronomy)|WD=Q943247|lt=Extinction (astronomy)|1=Extinction (astronomy)}}, Semed-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 3.5°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 21 to 24 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == Coordinates of Semed-seret (Equinox 1850 BCE) == * {{ill|Right ascension|en|Right ascension|WD=Q13442|lt=Right ascension|1=Right ascension}}: 16h39m45s * [[Declination (astronomy)|Declination]]: −15°05'46" == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Semed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[ja:セメド・セレト]] [[de:Semed-seret]] {{wikidata link|Q1621224}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]
